title: First and last occurrence of a given number

content.problemStatement:
You are given a sorted array of integers called arrarrarr in ascending order. Your task is to find the first and last positions of a given integer keykeykey.


If keykeykey is not present in the array, return [−1,−1][-1, -1][−1,−1].
Your solution must have a runtime complexity of **O(log n)**.
Function DeclarationFunction NamesearchRangesearchRangesearchRange – This function finds the first and last occurrence of a given key in a sorted array.

Parameters
arrarrarr : A reference to a sorted array of integers (may contain duplicates).
keykeykey : The integer value whose first and last positions are to be found.
Return Value
Returns a vector of two integers:

First occurrence index of keykeykey
Last occurrence index of keykeykey


Returns [−1,−1][-1, -1][−1,−1] if keykeykey is not present.
Constraints
0≤arr.length≤1050 \leq arr.length \leq 10^50≤arr.length≤105
−109≤arr[i]≤109-10^9 \leq arr[i] \leq 10^9−109≤arr[i]≤109
arrarrarr is sorted in **non-decreasing order**
−109≤key≤109-10^9 \leq key \leq 10^9−109≤key≤109

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT — number of test cases.
For each test case:

One line containing two integers: nnn and keykeykey
One line containing nnn space-separated integers — the sorted array




Output Format

For each test case, print two integers:

first occurrence index and last occurrence index
constraints: arr is sorted in non‑decreasing order
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers n and key, where n is the size of the sorted array (may be zero) and key is the target value. The second line contains n space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order. If n is zero the second line is empty.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two integers separated by a space: the index of the first occurrence of key and the index of the last occurrence of key in the array. Use 0‑based indexing. If key does not appear, output "-1 -1".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test: 5 appears at indices 3 and 5. Second test: 7 is absent. Third test: array is empty, so -1 -1.
In the first case 3 occurs from index 2 to 4. In the second case 7 does not exist.
Only one occurrence of 5 at index 4.

solution.approach: Because the array is sorted we can locate the first and last occurrence of the target with two independent binary searches. The first search moves the right bound leftwards when the target is found, thus remembering the leftmost index. The second search moves the left bound rightwards when the target is found, thus remembering the rightmost index. Both runs take O(log n) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log n) per test case, total O(T·log n).
